rulings.Case Title: LESLIE MORRIS et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIAL COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 6, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. By failing to submit verified factually devoid responses with its moving papers, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff objected on the grounds of no verifications. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) Defendant failed to sustain its burden under the negation of an element prong of Aguilar because defendant did not submit admissible evidence negating an essential element of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE MORRIS et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIAL COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 6, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Nibco, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of issues 1, 2 and 3 (false representation, intentional tort and punitive damages) are denied. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Ms. Morris¿ exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to her asbestos-related disease. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Specifically, plaintiff identified himself as a person with knowledge supporting his claim for negligence, defendant¿s special interrogatories did not ask what knowledge he had and defendant failed to demonstrate that Mr. Garrett does not have knowledge supporting his claim for negligence. As to summary adjudication of issues 1, 2 and 3, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting his false representation and intentional tort causes of action and claim for punitive damages. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Specifically, plaintiff identified himself as a person with knowledge supporting these claims, defendant¿s special interrogatories did not ask what knowledge he had and defendant failed to demonstrate that Mr. Garrett does not have knowledge supporting his false representation and intentional tort causes of action and claim for punitive damages. Plaintiff¿s request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
